description: Techniques for modeling systems in which uncertainty is an essential factor. Emphasizes why, how and when techniques can or cannot be applied, rather than their mathematical derivation. Case studies and/or examples from such areas as logistics, production, and service industries.

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: (STAT/​MATH  309,311, orSTAT/​MATH  431) and (MATH 320,340,341, or375), graduate/professional standing, or member of Engineering Guest Students
